sections.paragraph:
πλέθρον (πλέθρον, ου, τό,)

I. as measure of length, a plethron, = 100 Greek or 101 English feet, 1/6 of a stade, Hdt., Xen.

II. as a square measure, 10, 000 square feet (Greek) = about 37 perches, Plat., Dem.;—used to translate the Rom. jugerum, though this was about 2 roods 19 perches, Plut.


Etym. deriv. uncertain
